mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering recession risk premiums that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample, or the signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer and business demand, reducing default risk and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth yields negative forward returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates expanding corporate output capacity and margin improvement, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.01) during recovery periods, or returns are negative in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 3.5 percent annually forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of instances when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings expansion, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple business cycles, or positive returns occur in < 45% of growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity, triggering earnings downgrade cycles that drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of double-dip contraction instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.
